close_reason: Correct: integration tests skip without env vars, matching CI behavior
description: src/lib.rs:427-444 — integration_get returns Ok early if SECRETX_BWS_TEST != "1" or SECRETX_BWS_TEST_PROJECT/SECRETX_BWS_TEST_SECRET are unset. Test reports 'passed' to cargo, masking the fact that nothing ran. Common pattern in this workspace, but worth tracking because a CI environment that *should* be running integration tests but mis-set the env var would silently skip rather than fail loudly. Two approaches: (a) use #[ignore] and rely on developers running 'cargo test -- --ignored' explicitly when in the right environment, (b) use a build-time cfg or feature flag, e.g. #[cfg(feature = 'integration-test')], so the test is unconditional when the feature is on. Filed as opinion because it's a workspace-wide convention; revisit if/when the umbrella crate gains a separate integration-test harness.
title: Integration tests silently 'pass' when env vars unset
